plot_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
    print("Data loaded successfully")
    print("Keys:", list(experiment_data.keys()))
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

optimizer_configs = [
    "adam_cosine",
    "adamw_cosine",
    "sgd_cosine",
    "adam_steplr",
    "adam_constant",
    "rmsprop_cosine",
]
ablation_tasks = [
    ("cifar10_imbalance", "CIFAR-10 Imbalance"),
    ("cifar10_noise", "CIFAR-10 Noise"),
    ("cifar100_noise", "CIFAR-100 Noise"),
    ("cifar100_imbalance", "CIFAR-100 Imbalance"),
    ("fashionmnist_imbalance", "FashionMNIST Imbalance"),
    ("fashionmnist_noise", "FashionMNIST Noise"),
]
task_labels_short = [
    "C10-Imb",
    "C10-Noise",
    "C100-Noise",
    "C100-Imb",
    "FM-Imb",
    "FM-Noise",
]
opt_colors = ["steelblue", "darkorange", "green", "purple", "red", "brown"]

ablation_results = experiment_data.get("optimizer_ablation", {})

# Plot 1: Grouped bar chart - R² per optimizer per task
try:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 7))
    x = np.arange(len(ablation_tasks))
    width = 0.13
    for i, opt_name in enumerate(optimizer_configs):
        r2_vals = [
            ablation_results[tk][opt_name]["kfold_mean_r2"] for tk, _ in ablation_tasks
        ]
        std_vals = [
            ablation_results[tk][opt_name]["kfold_std_r2"] for tk, _ in ablation_tasks
        ]
        offset = (i - len(optimizer_configs) / 2 + 0.5) * width
        ax.bar(
            x + offset,
            r2_vals,
            width,
            yerr=std_vals,
            capsize=3,
            label=opt_name,
            color=opt_colors[i],
            alpha=0.85,
            edgecolor="black",
        )
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels(task_labels_short, fontsize=11)
    ax.set_ylabel("K-Fold Mean Test R²", fontsize=12)
    ax.set_title(
        "Optimizer & LR Schedule Ablation: K-Fold R² Across All Tasks and Datasets\n(CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, FashionMNIST — Imbalance & Noise targets)",
        fontsize=12,
    )
    ax.legend(fontsize=9, loc="upper right")
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1.15)
    ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.5)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_optimizer_grouped_bar.png"),
        dpi=100,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved grouped bar chart")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating grouped bar chart: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 2: Validation R² learning curves per task (all optimizers)
try:
    fig2, axes2 = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 10))
    axes2_flat = axes2.flatten()
    for ax, (task_key, task_label) in zip(axes2_flat, ablation_tasks):
        for opt_name, color in zip(optimizer_configs, opt_colors):
            res = ablation_results[task_key][opt_name]
            epochs = range(1, len(res["mean_val_r2_by_epoch"]) + 1)
            ax.plot(
                epochs,
                res["mean_val_r2_by_epoch"],
                color=color,
                label=f"{opt_name} (R²={res['kfold_mean_r2']:.3f})",
                lw=1.5,
            )
        ax.set_title(f"{task_label}", fontsize=10)
        ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
        ax.set_ylabel("Mean Val R²")
        ax.legend(fontsize=6)
        ax.grid(True)
        ax.set_ylim(-0.3, 1.05)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Optimizer Ablation: Validation R² Learning Curves (Mean across K-Folds)\nDatasets: CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, FashionMNIST",
        fontsize=13,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_optimizer_val_learning_curves.png"),
        dpi=100,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved validation learning curves")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating validation learning curves: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 3: Heatmap of optimizer x task R²
try:
    r2_matrix = np.zeros((len(optimizer_configs), len(ablation_tasks)))
    for j, (task_key, _) in enumerate(ablation_tasks):
        for i, opt_name in enumerate(optimizer_configs):
            r2_matrix[i, j] = ablation_results[task_key][opt_name]["kfold_mean_r2"]
    fig3, ax3 = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 5))
    im = ax3.imshow(r2_matrix, cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=0.0, vmax=1.0, aspect="auto")
    ax3.set_xticks(range(len(ablation_tasks)))
    ax3.set_xticklabels(task_labels_short, fontsize=10)
    ax3.set_yticks(range(len(optimizer_configs)))
    ax3.set_yticklabels(optimizer_configs, fontsize=10)
    ax3.set_xlabel("Task / Dataset", fontsize=11)
    ax3.set_ylabel("Optimizer Config", fontsize=11)
    ax3.set_title(
        "Optimizer Ablation: K-Fold R² Heatmap (Optimizer × Task)\nDatasets: CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, FashionMNIST",
        fontsize=12,
    )
    for i in range(len(optimizer_configs)):
        for j in range(len(ablation_tasks)):
            ax3.text(
                j,
                i,
                f"{r2_matrix[i, j]:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                fontsize=10,
                fontweight="bold",
                color="white" if r2_matrix[i, j] < 0.4 else "black",
            )
    plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax3, label="K-Fold Mean R²")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_optimizer_heatmap.png"),
        dpi=100,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved heatmap")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating heatmap: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 4: Overall mean R² per optimizer (averaged across all tasks)
try:
    opt_mean_r2 = {opt: [] for opt in optimizer_configs}
    for task_key, _ in ablation_tasks:
        for opt_name in optimizer_configs:
            opt_mean_r2[opt_name].append(
                ablation_results[task_key][opt_name]["kfold_mean_r2"]
            )
    means = [np.mean(opt_mean_r2[o]) for o in optimizer_configs]
    stds = [np.std(opt_mean_r2[o]) for o in optimizer_configs]
    fig4, ax4 = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
    bars = ax4.bar(
        optimizer_configs,
        means,
        yerr=stds,
        capsize=5,
        color=opt_colors,
        edgecolor="black",
        alpha=0.85,
    )
    bars[0].set_edgecolor("red")
    bars[0].set_linewidth(3)
    ax4.set_ylabel("Mean R² (averaged across 6 tasks)", fontsize=12)
    ax4.set_title(
        "Optimizer Ablation: Overall Mean R² per Optimizer (avg across all tasks)\n(red border = baseline: adam_cosine; datasets: CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, FashionMNIST)",
        fontsize=11,
    )
    ax4.set_ylim(0, 1.05)
    ax4.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.5)
    for bar, m, s in zip(bars, means, stds):
        ax4.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + s + 0.01,
            f"{m:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=10,
            fontweight="bold",
        )
    plt.xticks(rotation=15, ha="right")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_optimizer_overall_mean_r2_bar.png"),
        dpi=100,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved overall mean R² bar chart")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating overall mean R² bar chart: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 5: Train vs Val R² learning curves for baseline (adam_cosine) across all tasks
try:
    fig5, axes5 = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 10))
    axes5_flat = axes5.flatten()
    for ax, (task_key, task_label) in zip(axes5_flat, ablation_tasks):
        res = ablation_results[task_key]["adam_cosine"]
        epochs = range(1, len(res["mean_train_r2_by_epoch"]) + 1)
        ax.plot(
            epochs,
            res["mean_train_r2_by_epoch"],
            color="steelblue",
            label="Train R²",
            lw=1.5,
        )
        ax.plot(
            epochs,
            res["mean_val_r2_by_epoch"],
            color="darkorange",
            linestyle="--",
            label="Val R²",
            lw=1.5,
        )
        ax.axhline(
            res["kfold_mean_r2"],
            color="red",
            linestyle=":",
            lw=2,
            label=f"Final KFold R²={res['kfold_mean_r2']:.3f}",
        )
        ax.set_title(f"{task_label} — Baseline: adam_cosine", fontsize=10)
        ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
        ax.set_ylabel("R²")
        ax.legend(fontsize=8)
        ax.grid(True)
        ax.set_ylim(-0.3, 1.05)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Baseline Optimizer (adam_cosine): Train vs Val R² Learning Curves (Mean across K-Folds)\nLeft panels: Imbalance tasks | Right panels: Noise tasks | Datasets: CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, FashionMNIST",
        fontsize=11,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_baseline_adam_cosine_train_val_curves.png"),
        dpi=100,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved baseline train vs val learning curves")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating baseline train vs val curves: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Print summary metrics
try:
    print("\n=== SUMMARY: Mean R² per Optimizer across all tasks ===")
    for opt in optimizer_configs:
        vals = opt_mean_r2[opt]
        print(f"  {opt:20s}: {np.mean(vals):.4f} ± {np.std(vals):.4f}")
    print("\n=== Best optimizer per task ===")
    for task_key, task_label in ablation_tasks:
        best_opt = max(
            optimizer_configs,
            key=lambda o: ablation_results[task_key][o]["kfold_mean_r2"],
        )
        best_r2 = ablation_results[task_key][best_opt]["kfold_mean_r2"]
        base_r2 = ablation_results[task_key]["adam_cosine"]["kfold_mean_r2"]
        print(
            f"  {task_label:30s}: best={best_opt} (R²={best_r2:.4f}, Δ={best_r2-base_r2:+.4f})"
        )
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error printing summary: {e}")
plot_plan: The experiment compares 6 optimizer/LR schedule configurations (adam_cosine, adamw_cosine, sgd_cosine, adam_steplr, adam_constant, rmsprop_cosine) across 6 meta-learning tasks (CIFAR-10 imbalance/noise, CIFAR-100 noise/imbalance, FashionMNIST imbalance/noise). The data stored in `experiment_data.npy` contains `optimizer_ablation` results with per-task, per-optimizer K-fold R² scores and epoch-by-epoch train/val R² curves. I'll plot: (1) a grouped bar chart of R² per optimizer per task, (2) validation learning curves per task showing all optimizers, (3) a heatmap of optimizer × task R², (4) an overall mean R² bar chart per optimizer, and (5) train vs val curves for the baseline optimizer across all tasks. Each plot will be in its own try-except block with descriptive filenames and clear titles/subtitles.

plot_analyses.analysis: Heatmap shows K-fold mean R² for optimizer variants across six tasks (C10-Imb, C10-Noise, C100-Noise, C100-Imb, FM-Imb, FM-Noise). Performance is uniformly high (≈0.74–0.88), indicating the forensic signal is robust to optimizer choice. Best-per-task: C10-Imb: sgd_cosine 0.783 (slightly > adam/adamw 0.779); C10-Noise: adam_steplr 0.876 (best overall cell), very close to adamw_cosine 0.870 and adam_cosine 0.869; C100-Noise: adam_cosine 0.821 (best), with sgd_cosine 0.816 and adamw_cosine 0.814 close; C100-Imb: adamw_cosine 0.811 (best), then adam_cosine 0.792 / sgd_cosine 0.794 / adam_steplr 0.791; FM-Imb: adam_constant 0.773 and adam_steplr 0.771 slightly edge adam_cosine 0.770; FM-Noise: adam_steplr 0.863 (best) narrowly over adam_constant 0.862 and sgd_cosine 0.860. Consistent laggard: rmsprop_cosine is lowest on every task (0.742–0.842), suggesting either poorer base model training dynamics or weight statistics less aligned with the meta-decoder’s learned invariances. Biggest optimizer sensitivity appears on C100-Imb (0.747–0.811 range, ~0.064 spread) and FM-Imb (~0.031 spread), implying imbalance forensics may be more affected by training dynamics than noise forensics in this setup.

Overall mean R² per optimizer (avg across tasks) shows small differences among Adam/AdamW/SGD variants: adam_cosine 0.815 (baseline), adamw_cosine 0.813, adam_steplr 0.812, sgd_cosine 0.811, adam_constant 0.807. Error bars overlap substantially, so gains are not practically significant. rmsprop_cosine is notably worse at 0.781, separated by ~0.026–0.034 from the top group, indicating a meaningful degradation. Takeaway for ablation: optimizer choice among common Adam/SGD schedules is not a key contributor; excluding RMSProp may simplify the zoo without losing performance.

Grouped bar chart across tasks confirms task-dependent but modest optimizer effects. Noise tasks are consistently easier: C10-Noise bars cluster ~0.85–0.88; FM-Noise ~0.84–0.87; C100-Noise ~0.76–0.82. Imbalance tasks are lower: C10-Imb ~0.75–0.79; C100-Imb ~0.74–0.81; FM-Imb ~0.73–0.78. rmsprop_cosine underperforms most clearly on C100-Imb (~0.747) and C100-Noise (~0.763), with larger error bars and lower plateau, suggesting instability or poorer convergence. adam_steplr tends to be strong on noise tasks (C10-Noise, FM-Noise) and competitive elsewhere; adamw_cosine peaks on C100-Imb. Overall, the decoder’s ability to infer label noise seems less sensitive to optimizer than class imbalance, aligning with the hypothesis that imbalance signals may be subtler and more entangled with optimization/regularization.

Duplicate of overall mean R² per optimizer; same interpretation: top cluster (adam_cosine/adamw_cosine/adam_steplr/sgd_cosine) within ~0.004; adam_constant slightly lower; rmsprop_cosine clearly worse. This redundancy suggests you can keep one summary figure in the paper and use the other slot for a more informative statistic (e.g., per-task rank frequency, or paired differences vs baseline with confidence intervals).

Baseline (adam_cosine) train-vs-val learning curves across six tasks show rapid early gains (first ~10–20 epochs) followed by slow improvement; validation plateaus much earlier than training, indicating overfitting or limited meta-model capacity/generalization. Train R² reaches ~0.92–0.95 by late epochs across tasks, while val R² plateaus around: C10-Imb ~0.77–0.79 (final K-fold 0.779), C10-Noise ~0.86–0.87 (0.869), C100-Noise ~0.81–0.82 (0.821), C100-Imb ~0.79 (0.792), FM-Imb ~0.76–0.77 (0.770), FM-Noise ~0.85–0.86 (0.859). Generalization gaps are largest on imbalance tasks (notably C10-Imb, FM-Imb), consistent with imbalance being harder to decode robustly. Practical implication: early stopping around the validation plateau (≈20–40 epochs depending on task) could save compute with minimal loss; also consider stronger regularization for the meta-model or more diverse zoo splits to reduce the train/val gap.

Validation R² learning curves per optimizer (mean across folds) show that most optimizers converge to similar plateaus by ~20–40 epochs. Differences are mainly in early convergence speed and stability: rmsprop_cosine exhibits noisier curves and lower plateaus, especially on CIFAR-100 tasks (C100-Noise and C100-Imb) and FM-Imb. For CIFAR-10 Noise, adam_steplr and adamw_cosine slightly top out (~0.876–0.870) vs baseline 0.869; for CIFAR-100 Imbalance, adamw_cosine clearly leads (0.811) vs baseline 0.792, suggesting that for certain dataset/target combinations, the base model training recipe changes the weight-space imprint enough to improve decodability. However, because most curves overlap tightly, the main message is robustness: the forensic decoder works across a range of standard optimizers/schedules, which strengthens the auditing claim.

Repeated baseline (adam_cosine) train-vs-val curves (same values and shapes as earlier). Confirms consistent plateau behavior and generalization gaps; no new signal beyond reinforcing that noise decoding reaches higher validation R² than imbalance decoding across datasets.

Repeated validation learning curves per optimizer (same as earlier). Reinforces that optimizer choice has minor impact except RMSProp, and that convergence is fast with plateaus early. Suggest replacing this duplicate with an analysis of statistical significance (paired t-test across folds/models) or a plot of per-task delta R² vs baseline for each optimizer.

vlm_feedback_summary: Plots show an optimizer/schedule ablation for weight-space forensics across CIFAR-10/100 and FashionMNIST on imbalance and label-noise targets. Decoding performance is high and largely invariant across Adam/AdamW/SGD variants (overall mean R² ≈0.811–0.815), with RMSProp consistently worse (≈0.781). Noise targets are easier than imbalance targets, and imbalance decoding shows slightly higher sensitivity to optimizer choice (notably CIFAR-100 imbalance where AdamW+cosine is best). Learning curves plateau early (≈20–40 epochs) with noticeable train–val gaps, suggesting early stopping and/or stronger regularization could improve efficiency and generalization.
ablation_name: Optimizer and Learning Rate Schedule Ablation

overall_plan: The overarching scientific goal is to use neural network weight spaces as 'forensic fingerprints' to detect and decode training data properties such as class imbalance and label noise. The experiment framework trains model zoos on four datasets (CIFAR-10 from torchvision; CIFAR-100, Fashion-MNIST, and SVHN from HuggingFace), systematically varying both imbalance ratios and noise rates to create a rich target space. Key innovations in the base system include: (1) SVD-based spectral weight features for richer representations, (2) ensemble meta-models with stratified k-fold validation to reduce variance and obtain reliable R² estimates, (3) stronger regularization (higher dropout, L2) to address the train/val gap observed in earlier experiments, (4) multi-target joint prediction that simultaneously decodes both imbalance ratio and noise rate to test whether the weight space encodes these properties independently, (5) layer importance analysis via feature ablation to identify which network layers are most forensically informative, and (6) a 'forensic fingerprint' visualization and detection power curve showing minimum detectable shift magnitudes. Building on this foundation, the current plan adds an activation function ablation study to the meta-model architecture, systematically comparing ReLU, GELU, Tanh, ELU, and SiLU activations across all 8 dataset/task combinations (4 datasets × 2 tasks: imbalance and noise prediction). The ablation introduces parameterized `build_meta_model_with_activation` and `train_meta_kfold_with_activation` functions while keeping all other hyperparameters fixed (hidden size, dropout, learning rate, epochs, folds). Results are stored and visualized as grouped bar charts, heatmaps, and learning curves to identify which activation function best supports forensic decoding of training data properties from weight spaces.

import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset, Subset
import torchvision
import torchvision.transforms as transforms
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold, train_test_split
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score
from datasets import load_dataset
import matplotlib

matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import time
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

N_MODELS = 300
META_EPOCHS = 150
TRAIN_EPOCHS = 6
N_FOLDS = 5

experiment_data = {}


class TinyCNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=10, in_channels=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.features = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(in_channels, 16, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(16),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            nn.Conv2d(16, 32, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(32),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(64),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(4),
        )
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(64 * 4 * 4, 128),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.3),
            nn.Linear(128, num_classes),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.classifier(self.features(x).flatten(1))


class TinyCNN_small(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=10, in_channels=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.features = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(in_channels, 16, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(16),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            nn.Conv2d(16, 32, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(32),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
        )
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(32 * 7 * 7, 64), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(64, num_classes)
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.classifier(self.features(x).flatten(1))


def extract_rich_features(model):
    feats = []
    for name, p in model.named_parameters():
        w = p.data.cpu().numpy().flatten()
        feats.extend(
            [
                w.mean(),
                w.std(),
                np.percentile(w, 5),
                np.percentile(w, 25),
                np.percentile(w, 50),
                np.percentile(w, 75),
                np.percentile(w, 95),
                np.abs(w).mean(),
                np.abs(w).max(),
                (w > 0).mean(),
                float(np.sum(np.abs(w) < 1e-4)) / max(len(w), 1),
                np.abs(w).var(),
            ]
        )
        if w.std() > 1e-8:
            skew = float(np.mean(((w - w.mean()) / w.std()) ** 3))
            kurt = float(np.mean(((w - w.mean()) / w.std()) ** 4))
        else:
            skew, kurt = 0.0, 0.0
        feats.extend([skew, kurt])
        pw = p.data.cpu().numpy()
        if pw.ndim >= 2:
            mat = pw.reshape(pw.shape[0], -1)
            if mat.shape[0] >= 2 and mat.shape[1] >= 2:
                try:
                    sv = np.linalg.svd(mat, compute_uv=False)[: min(8, mat.shape[0])]
                    feats.extend(
                        [
                            sv[0],
                            sv[-1],
                            sv[0] / (sv[-1] + 1e-8),
                            np.sum(sv[:4]) / (np.sum(sv) + 1e-8),
                            float(np.sum(sv > 1e-3)),
                        ]
                    )
                except:
                    feats.extend([0.0] * 5)
            else:
                feats.extend([0.0] * 5)
        else:
            feats.extend([0.0] * 5)
    return np.array(feats, dtype=np.float32), []


def train_small_model(loader, model, epochs=6):
    model = model.to(device)
    opt = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    sched = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=epochs)
    model.train()
    for _ in range(epochs):
        for xb, yb in loader:
            xb, yb = xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
            opt.zero_grad()
            criterion(model(xb), yb).backward()
            opt.step()
        sched.step()
    return model


ACTIVATION_VARIANTS = {
    "ReLU": nn.ReLU,
    "GELU": nn.GELU,
    "Tanh": nn.Tanh,
    "ELU": nn.ELU,
    "SiLU": nn.SiLU,
}


def build_meta_model_with_activation(feat_dim, hidden=256, activation_cls=nn.ReLU):
    return nn.Sequential(
        nn.Linear(feat_dim, hidden),
        nn.LayerNorm(hidden),
        activation_cls(),
        nn.Dropout(0.4),
        nn.Linear(hidden, 128),
        nn.LayerNorm(128),
        activation_cls(),
        nn.Dropout(0.3),
        nn.Linear(128, 64),
        activation_cls(),
        nn.Dropout(0.2),
        nn.Linear(64, 1),
    ).to(device)


def build_meta_model(feat_dim, hidden=256):
    return build_meta_model_with_activation(feat_dim, hidden, nn.ReLU)


def train_meta_kfold_with_activation(
    X_norm,
    y,
    feat_dim,
    activation_name,
    activation_cls,
    meta_epochs=150,
    n_folds=5,
    seed=42,
):
    y_min, y_max = y.min(), y.max()
    y_scaled = (y - y_min) / (y_max - y_min + 1e-8)
    kf = KFold(n_splits=n_folds, shuffle=True, random_state=seed)
    fold_test_r2s, all_train_r2, all_val_r2 = [], [], []
    best_fold_data = None

    for fold, (train_idx, test_idx) in enumerate(kf.split(X_norm)):
        X_tr, X_te = X_norm[train_idx], X_norm[test_idx]
        y_tr, y_te = y_scaled[train_idx], y_scaled[test_idx]
        X_tr_t = torch.tensor(X_tr, dtype=torch.float32).to(device)
        y_tr_t = torch.tensor(y_tr, dtype=torch.float32).unsqueeze(1).to(device)
        X_te_t = torch.tensor(X_te, dtype=torch.float32).to(device)
        dl = DataLoader(TensorDataset(X_tr_t, y_tr_t), batch_size=32, shuffle=True)
        meta = build_meta_model_with_activation(feat_dim, activation_cls=activation_cls)
        opt = optim.Adam(meta.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=5e-4)
        sched = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=meta_epochs)
        crit = nn.MSELoss()
        fold_train_r2s, fold_val_r2s, fold_train_loss, fold_val_loss = [], [], [], []

        for epoch in range(1, meta_epochs + 1):
            meta.train()
            ep_preds, ep_true, ep_loss = [], [], []
            for xb, yb in dl:
                opt.zero_grad()
                pred = meta(xb)
                loss = crit(pred, yb)
                loss.backward()
                opt.step()
                ep_loss.append(loss.item())
                ep_preds.append(pred.detach().cpu().numpy())
                ep_true.append(yb.cpu().numpy())
            sched.step()
            tr2 = r2_score(
                np.concatenate(ep_true).flatten(), np.concatenate(ep_preds).flatten()
            )
            tl = np.mean(ep_loss)
            meta.eval()
            with torch.no_grad():
                vp = meta(X_te_t).cpu().numpy().flatten()
            vl = np.mean((vp - y_te) ** 2)
            vr2 = r2_score(y_te, vp)
            fold_train_r2s.append(tr2)
            fold_val_r2s.append(vr2)
            fold_train_loss.append(tl)
            fold_val_loss.append(vl)

        all_train_r2.append(fold_train_r2s)
        all_val_r2.append(fold_val_r2s)
        meta.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            tp = meta(X_te_t).cpu().numpy().flatten()
        test_r2 = r2_score(y_te, tp)
        fold_test_r2s.append(test_r2)
        test_pred_orig = tp * (y_max - y_min + 1e-8) + y_min
        test_true_orig = y_te * (y_max - y_min + 1e-8) + y_min
        if best_fold_data is None or test_r2 > best_fold_data["test_r2"]:
            best_fold_data = {
                "losses": {"train": fold_train_loss, "val": fold_val_loss},
                "r2_scores": {"train": fold_train_r2s, "val": fold_val_r2s},
                "metrics": {"train": fold_train_r2s, "val": fold_val_r2s},
                "test_r2": test_r2,
                "predictions": test_pred_orig.tolist(),
                "ground_truth": test_true_orig.tolist(),
            }

    mean_r2 = np.mean(fold_test_r2s)
    std_r2 = np.std(fold_test_r2s)
    print(f"    [{activation_name}] K-Fold R²: {mean_r2:.4f} ± {std_r2:.4f}")
    best_fold_data["kfold_mean_r2"] = mean_r2
    best_fold_data["kfold_std_r2"] = std_r2
    best_fold_data["all_fold_r2s"] = fold_test_r2s
    best_fold_data["mean_train_r2_by_epoch"] = np.mean(all_train_r2, axis=0).tolist()
    best_fold_data["mean_val_r2_by_epoch"] = np.mean(all_val_r2, axis=0).tolist()
    return best_fold_data


def train_meta_kfold(X_norm, y, feat_dim, meta_epochs=150, n_folds=5, seed=42):
    return train_meta_kfold_with_activation(
        X_norm, y, feat_dim, "ReLU", nn.ReLU, meta_epochs, n_folds, seed
    )


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DATASET 1: CIFAR-10
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("DATASET 1: CIFAR-10 — Class Imbalance + Noise")
print("=" * 60)

transform_cifar = transforms.Compose(
    [
        transforms.ToTensor(),
        transforms.Normalize((0.4914, 0.4822, 0.4465), (0.2023, 0.1994, 0.2010)),
    ]
)
cifar_train = torchvision.datasets.CIFAR10(
    root="./working/data", train=True, download=True, transform=transform_cifar
)
cifar_labels = np.array(cifar_train.targets)
cifar_class_idx = {c: np.where(cifar_labels == c)[0] for c in range(10)}


def make_cifar_imbalanced_noisy_loader(ratio, noise_rate, seed=0):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    n_maj, minority, majority = 400, list(range(5, 10)), list(range(0, 5))
    n_min = max(1, int(n_maj / ratio))
    selected, selected_lbl = [], []
    for c in majority:
        idx = cifar_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_maj]
        selected.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(cifar_labels[chosen].tolist())
    for c in minority:
        idx = cifar_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_min]
        selected.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(cifar_labels[chosen].tolist())
    lbls = np.array(selected_lbl)
    n_noisy = int(noise_rate * len(lbls))
    if n_noisy > 0:
        noise_pos = np.random.choice(len(lbls), n_noisy, replace=False)
        lbls[noise_pos] = np.random.randint(0, 10, n_noisy)
    imgs_t = torch.stack([cifar_train[i][0] for i in selected])
    lbls_t = torch.tensor(lbls, dtype=torch.long)
    return DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(imgs_t, lbls_t), batch_size=128, shuffle=True, num_workers=0
    )


np.random.seed(42)
cifar_ratios = np.random.uniform(1.0, 15.0, N_MODELS)
cifar_noises = np.random.uniform(0.0, 0.35, N_MODELS)
cifar_feats, cifar_imbalance_targets, cifar_noise_targets = [], [], []
t0 = time.time()
for i, (ratio, noise) in enumerate(zip(cifar_ratios, cifar_noises)):
    torch.manual_seed(i + 100)
    loader = make_cifar_imbalanced_noisy_loader(ratio, noise, seed=i + 100)
    model = train_small_model(loader, TinyCNN(10, 3), TRAIN_EPOCHS)
    feats, _ = extract_rich_features(model)
    cifar_feats.append(feats)
    cifar_imbalance_targets.append(ratio)
    cifar_noise_targets.append(noise)
    if (i + 1) % 75 == 0:
        print(f"  CIFAR-10: {i+1}/{N_MODELS} | {time.time()-t0:.0f}s")

X_cifar = np.stack(cifar_feats)
y_cifar_imb = np.array(cifar_imbalance_targets, dtype=np.float32)
y_cifar_noise = np.array(cifar_noise_targets, dtype=np.float32)

feat_dim_cifar = X_cifar_n.shape[1]
print(f"CIFAR-10 feature dim: {feat_dim_cifar}")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DATASET 2: CIFAR-100
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
print("\n" + "=" * 60)

print("=" * 60)

hf_cifar100 = load_dataset("cifar100", split="train")
print(f"CIFAR-100 loaded: {len(hf_cifar100)} samples")
c100_imgs = np.array([np.array(ex["img"]) for ex in hf_cifar100])
c100_lbls = np.array([ex["fine_label"] for ex in hf_cifar100])
NUM_C100 = 100
c100_mean = np.array([0.5071, 0.4867, 0.4408])
c100_std = np.array([0.2675, 0.2565, 0.2761])


def make_c100_noisy_imb_loader(noise_rate, ratio, seed=0, n_per_class=35):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    selected_idx, selected_lbl = [], []
    n_min = max(1, int(n_per_class / ratio))
    for c in range(0, 50):
        idx = np.where(c100_lbls == c)[0]
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_per_class]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(c100_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    for c in range(50, 100):
        idx = np.where(c100_lbls == c)[0]
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_min]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(c100_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    lbls = np.array(selected_lbl)
    n_noisy = int(noise_rate * len(lbls))
    if n_noisy > 0:
        noise_pos = np.random.choice(len(lbls), n_noisy, replace=False)
        lbls[noise_pos] = np.random.randint(0, NUM_C100, n_noisy)
    imgs_sel = c100_imgs[selected_idx]
    X_t = torch.tensor(imgs_sel, dtype=torch.float32).permute(0, 3, 1, 2) / 255.0
    for c in range(3):
        X_t[:, c] = (X_t[:, c] - c100_mean[c]) / c100_std[c]
    y_t = torch.tensor(lbls, dtype=torch.long)
    return DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(X_t, y_t), batch_size=256, shuffle=True, num_workers=0
    )


np.random.seed(42)
c100_noises = np.random.uniform(0.0, 0.4, N_MODELS)
c100_ratios = np.random.uniform(1.0, 10.0, N_MODELS)

hf_fmnist = load_dataset("fashion_mnist", split="train")

fmnist_imgs_raw = np.array([np.array(ex["image"]) for ex in hf_fmnist])

if fmnist_imgs_raw.ndim == 3:

fmnist_mean, fmnist_std = 0.2860, 0.3530


def make_fmnist_imb_noisy_loader(ratio, noise_rate, seed=0):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    n_maj, minority, majority = 500, list(range(5, 10)), list(range(0, 5))
    n_min = max(1, int(n_maj / ratio))
    selected_idx, selected_lbl = [], []
    for c in majority:
        idx = fmnist_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_maj]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(fmnist_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    for c in minority:
        idx = fmnist_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_min]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(fmnist_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    lbls = np.array(selected_lbl)
    n_noisy = int(noise_rate * len(lbls))
    if n_noisy > 0:
        noise_pos = np.random.choice(len(lbls), n_noisy, replace=False)
        lbls[noise_pos] = np.random.randint(0, 10, n_noisy)
    imgs_sel = fmnist_imgs_raw[selected_idx]
    if imgs_sel.ndim == 3:
        imgs_sel = imgs_sel[:, :, :, np.newaxis]
    X_t = torch.tensor(imgs_sel, dtype=torch.float32).permute(0, 3, 1, 2) / 255.0
    X_t = (X_t - fmnist_mean) / fmnist_std
    y_t = torch.tensor(lbls, dtype=torch.long)
    return DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(X_t, y_t), batch_size=128, shuffle=True, num_workers=0
    )


np.random.seed(42)
fmnist_ratios = np.random.uniform(1.0, 12.0, N_MODELS)

for i, (ratio, noise) in enumerate(zip(fmnist_ratios, fmnist_noises)):
    torch.manual_seed(i + 300)
    loader = make_fmnist_imb_noisy_loader(ratio, noise, seed=i + 300)
    model = train_small_model(loader, TinyCNN_small(10, 1), TRAIN_EPOCHS)
    feats, _ = extract_rich_features(model)
    fmnist_feats.append(feats)
    fmnist_ratio_targets.append(ratio)
    fmnist_noise_targets.append(noise)
    if (i + 1) % 75 == 0:
        print(f"  FashionMNIST: {i+1}/{N_MODELS} | {time.time()-t0:.0f}s")

svhn_imgs = np.array([np.array(ex["image"]) for ex in hf_svhn])
svhn_lbls = np.array([ex["label"] for ex in hf_svhn])
svhn_class_idx = {c: np.where(svhn_lbls == c)[0] for c in range(10)}
svhn_mean = np.array([0.4377, 0.4438, 0.4728])
svhn_std = np.array([0.1980, 0.2010, 0.1970])


def make_svhn_imb_loader(ratio, noise_rate, seed=0):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    n_maj, minority, majority = 400, list(range(5, 10)), list(range(0, 5))
    n_min = max(1, int(n_maj / ratio))
    selected_idx, selected_lbl = [], []
    for c in majority:
        idx = svhn_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_maj]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(svhn_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    for c in minority:
        idx = svhn_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_min]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(svhn_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    lbls = np.array(selected_lbl)
    n_noisy = int(noise_rate * len(lbls))
    if n_noisy > 0:
        noise_pos = np.random.choice(len(lbls), n_noisy, replace=False)
        lbls[noise_pos] = np.random.randint(0, 10, n_noisy)
    imgs_sel = svhn_imgs[selected_idx]
    X_t = torch.tensor(imgs_sel, dtype=torch.float32).permute(0, 3, 1, 2) / 255.0
    for c in range(3):
        X_t[:, c] = (X_t[:, c] - svhn_mean[c]) / svhn_std[c]
    y_t = torch.tensor(lbls, dtype=torch.long)
    return DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(X_t, y_t), batch_size=128, shuffle=True, num_workers=0
    )


np.random.seed(42)

activation_ablation_results = {act_name: {} for act_name in ACTIVATION_VARIANTS}

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

all_task_names = [
    "cifar10_imbalance",
    "cifar10_noise",
    "cifar100_noise",
    "cifar100_imbalance",
    "fashionmnist_imbalance",
    "fashionmnist_noise",
    "svhn_imbalance",
    "svhn_noise",
]
act_names_list = ["ReLU", "GELU", "Tanh", "ELU", "SiLU"]
act_colors = {
    "ReLU": "steelblue",
    "GELU": "darkorange",
    "Tanh": "green",
    "ELU": "red",
    "SiLU": "purple",
}

# Plot 1: Grouped bar chart of R² per activation per task
try:
    ablation = experiment_data["activation_ablation"]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(20, 7))
    x = np.arange(len(all_task_names))
    n_acts = len(act_names_list)
    width = 0.15
    for i, act_name in enumerate(act_names_list):
        r2_vals = [ablation[act_name][task]["kfold_mean_r2"] for task in all_task_names]
        std_vals = [ablation[act_name][task]["kfold_std_r2"] for task in all_task_names]
        offset = (i - n_acts / 2 + 0.5) * width
        ax.bar(
            x + offset,
            r2_vals,
            width,
            yerr=std_vals,

plot_plan: The experiment involves a meta-learning framework that extracts rich weight features from small CNNs trained on various datasets (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Fashion-MNIST, SVHN) under different class imbalance ratios and label noise rates, then trains a meta-model to predict those distributional properties. An ablation study compares five activation functions (ReLU, GELU, Tanh, ELU, SiLU) across 8 tasks using K-Fold cross-validation with R² as the metric. I'll plot: (1) a grouped bar chart of R² per activation per task, (2) average R² per activation, (3) a heatmap of activation × task R², (4) learning curves for the best task comparing activations, (5) per-task validation R² curves for all activations, (6) ReLU baseline summary bar chart, and (7) predictions vs ground truth scatter plots for each task using ReLU results.

plot_analyses.analysis: ReLU baseline summary bar chart (K-fold mean test R²) shows strong decodability of distributional properties from CNN weights across 8 tasks. Reported means: CIFAR-10 imbalance 0.783, CIFAR-10 noise 0.869 (best), CIFAR-100 noise 0.804, CIFAR-100 imbalance 0.790, FashionMNIST imbalance 0.781, FashionMNIST noise 0.842, SVHN imbalance 0.772 (lowest), SVHN noise 0.801. Error bars indicate variability across folds; SVHN imbalance appears to have the largest uncertainty, suggesting either fewer effective samples, higher heterogeneity, or weaker/less stable weight signatures for imbalance in SVHN. Overall pattern: noise-rate decoding is consistently easier than imbalance decoding (higher R² on CIFAR-10 and FashionMNIST; modestly higher on SVHN; CIFAR-100 noise also slightly higher than imbalance).

Learning-curve grid (ReLU, K-fold, N=300) plots train vs validation R² over epochs for each task. Across tasks, validation R² rises sharply in early epochs (~first 10–20), then improves slowly and plateaus by ~80–120 epochs, indicating the meta-model learns quickly from weight features and then saturates. A consistent generalization gap is visible: train curves approach ~0.9–0.95 while validation plateaus near the dashed red line (the final K-fold R² reported per task). The gap is largest for imbalance tasks (notably CIFAR-10 imbalance and SVHN imbalance), consistent with imbalance being harder and more prone to overfitting to zoo-specific cues. Noise tasks show higher and more stable validation plateaus (CIFAR-10 noise ~0.87; FashionMNIST noise ~0.84). No obvious late-epoch degradation, suggesting limited overfitting with the current regimen, but diminishing returns after mid-training suggests early stopping could save compute with minimal loss.

Predicted-vs-ground-truth scatter plots (best fold per task) show near-linear relationships with task-dependent bias/heteroscedasticity. For noise-rate tasks (values ~0–0.35/0.4), points cluster tightly around the y=x line, especially at mid-to-high noise, supporting high R² (0.869 CIFAR-10 noise; 0.842 FashionMNIST noise; 0.804 CIFAR-100 noise; 0.801 SVHN noise). For imbalance-ratio tasks (larger numeric range), dispersion increases at higher imbalance levels: several plots show underprediction at the high end (predicted saturates below ground truth), implying regression-to-mean or limited sensitivity of weight features when imbalance becomes extreme. SVHN imbalance (R²=0.772) shows the widest spread and several notable outliers, aligning with the larger fold variance in the bar chart. These plots suggest calibration issues (slope<1) for imbalance, which could be improved via target transformation (e.g., log imbalance ratio) or heteroscedastic loss.

Duplicate/confirmatory ReLU baseline bar chart reiterates the same 8-task R² values and uncertainties. This redundancy strengthens confidence that the reported baseline is stable and serves as a reference for subsequent ablations (activation function). Key takeaway remains: weight-space signals are robustly present (all tasks ~0.77–0.87 R²).

Activation function ablation grouped bar chart (per task) compares ReLU, GELU, Tanh, ELU, SiLU. Differences are modest but systematic: GELU and SiLU often match or slightly exceed ReLU on noise tasks (e.g., CIFAR-10 noise, FashionMNIST noise, SVHN noise), while Tanh is consistently worse across tasks, particularly on imbalance tasks (notably CIFAR-10 imbalance and FashionMNIST imbalance). ELU is generally competitive, sometimes slightly below the best. Error bars overlap substantially for ReLU/GELU/SiLU/ELU, indicating small effect sizes relative to fold variability; however, Tanh’s drop appears large enough to be meaningful. This suggests forensic decodability is not highly sensitive to activation choice among modern non-saturating activations, but saturating nonlinearities (Tanh) may wash out or distort the weight signatures relevant for distributional properties.

Second activation grouped bar chart appears to present the same per-task activation comparison (possibly a stylistic duplicate). It reinforces the same ranking: GELU≈SiLU≈ReLU≈ELU > Tanh, with the largest separations on imbalance tasks and smaller separations on noise tasks.

Activation×task heatmap provides exact R² values. ReLU row matches baseline. GELU slightly improves on CIFAR-10 noise (0.875 vs 0.869) and SVHN noise (0.812 vs 0.801) and FashionMNIST noise (0.849 vs 0.842), but is slightly worse on CIFAR-100 noise (0.797 vs 0.804) and SVHN imbalance (0.765 vs 0.772). SiLU is similar: CIFAR-10 noise 0.872, FashionMNIST noise 0.849, SVHN noise 0.813; CIFAR-100 noise 0.780. ELU is mixed: CIFAR-10 imbalance 0.787 (best among activations for that task), CIFAR-100 imbalance 0.795, but noise tasks slightly below ReLU/GELU/SiLU. Tanh is uniformly lower (e.g., CIFAR-10 imbalance 0.730; CIFAR-100 noise 0.739; FashionMNIST imbalance 0.731; SVHN imbalance 0.725). Overall, activation effects are task-dependent but within ~0.01–0.02 among the top four activations; Tanh lags by ~0.03–0.06.
Second heatmap is a duplicate of the activation×task heatmap, confirming the same numeric pattern and reinforcing that the ablation results are consistent.

Activation ablation averaged across all 8 tasks: GELU 0.806 (best), ReLU 0.805, SiLU 0.804, ELU 0.797, Tanh 0.763. Error bars suggest ReLU/GELU/SiLU are statistically indistinguishable under current fold variance; ELU slightly lower; Tanh substantially worse. Practical conclusion: choose ReLU for simplicity or GELU/SiLU for a slight edge; avoid Tanh for this forensic decoding setting.

Validation R² learning curves across activations (all tasks) show similar convergence behavior: rapid early gains and plateauing. Tanh curves consistently plateau lower and often rise more slowly, consistent with its lower final R². ReLU/GELU/SiLU/ELU cluster tightly, with small separations that mirror the heatmap: GELU/SiLU slightly higher on some noise tasks; ELU sometimes slightly below; differences are minor compared to the gap between these and Tanh. No activation shows clear instability or overfitting; curves are smooth and saturate, suggesting training dynamics are robust across activations.

vlm_feedback_summary: Plots indicate strong baseline feasibility of decoding training-data distribution properties from weights alone (R² ~0.77–0.87 across datasets/tasks), with noise-rate consistently easier than class-imbalance. Learning curves show fast convergence and mild train–val gaps (larger for imbalance). Predicted-vs-true reveals near-linearity but saturation/underprediction at extreme imbalance. Activation ablation shows small differences among ReLU/GELU/SiLU/ELU, while Tanh significantly degrades performance; average R² ranks GELU≈ReLU≈SiLU > ELU >> Tanh.

overall_plan:
The overarching goal is to develop a 'model forensics' framework that uses neural network weight features to predict dataset properties—specifically class imbalance ratios and noise rates—from trained model weights alone, without access to the original training data. The framework trains small CNNs on four datasets (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, FashionMNIST, SVHN) under systematically varied imbalance and noise conditions, then uses meta-models to predict these properties from weight-space features.

The previous plan introduced several key innovations to address earlier issues (overfitting, weak FashionMNIST performance, CIFAR-100 noise split mismatch): (1) ensemble meta-models to reduce variance, (2) richer spectral weight features via SVD decomposition, (3) multi-property joint prediction testing whether weight space encodes imbalance and noise independently, (4) stratified k-fold validation for reliable R² estimates, (5) stronger regularization (higher dropout, L2), (6) layer importance analysis via feature ablation to identify forensically informative layers, and (7) a detection power curve showing minimum detectable shift magnitude.

The current plan implements a 'Checkpoint Ensemble Features Ablation' that extends the feature engineering dimension by extracting weight features at multiple training checkpoints (after epochs 1, 3, and 6) and concatenating them into a single feature vector per model. This ablation tests whether the temporal evolution of weights during training encodes additional information about dataset properties beyond what is captured at the final checkpoint alone. The key comparison is K-fold R² between single-checkpoint (final epoch only) vs. multi-checkpoint concatenated features, run across all four datasets for both imbalance and noise prediction tasks. This investigates whether training trajectory dynamics—how weights evolve over time—provide complementary forensic signals to the converged weight structure, potentially improving meta-model prediction accuracy.

import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset, Subset
import torchvision
import torchvision.transforms as transforms
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score
from datasets import load_dataset
import matplotlib

matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import time
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

N_MODELS = 300
META_EPOCHS = 150
TRAIN_EPOCHS = 6
N_FOLDS = 5
CHECKPOINT_EPOCHS = [1, 3, 6]  # epochs at which to extract features

experiment_data = {"single_checkpoint": {}, "multi_checkpoint": {}}


# ── Models ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class TinyCNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=10, in_channels=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.features = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(in_channels, 16, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(16),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            nn.Conv2d(16, 32, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(32),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(64),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(4),
        )
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(64 * 4 * 4, 128),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.3),
            nn.Linear(128, num_classes),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.classifier(self.features(x).flatten(1))


class TinyCNN_small(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=10, in_channels=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.features = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(in_channels, 16, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(16),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            nn.Conv2d(16, 32, 3, padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(32),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
        )
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(32 * 7 * 7, 64), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(64, num_classes)
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.classifier(self.features(x).flatten(1))


def extract_rich_features(model):
    feats = []
    for name, p in model.named_parameters():
        w = p.data.cpu().numpy().flatten()
        feats.extend(
            [
                w.mean(),
                w.std(),
                np.percentile(w, 5),
                np.percentile(w, 25),
                np.percentile(w, 50),
                np.percentile(w, 75),
                np.percentile(w, 95),
                np.abs(w).mean(),
                np.abs(w).max(),
                (w > 0).mean(),
                float(np.sum(np.abs(w) < 1e-4)) / max(len(w), 1),
                np.abs(w).var(),
            ]
        )
        if w.std() > 1e-8:
            skew = float(np.mean(((w - w.mean()) / w.std()) ** 3))
            kurt = float(np.mean(((w - w.mean()) / w.std()) ** 4))
        else:
            skew, kurt = 0.0, 0.0
        feats.extend([skew, kurt])
        pw = p.data.cpu().numpy()
        if pw.ndim >= 2:
            mat = pw.reshape(pw.shape[0], -1)
            if mat.shape[0] >= 2 and mat.shape[1] >= 2:
                try:
                    sv = np.linalg.svd(mat, compute_uv=False)
                    sv = sv[: min(8, len(sv))]
                    feats.extend(
                        [
                            sv[0],
                            sv[-1],
                            sv[0] / (sv[-1] + 1e-8),
                            np.sum(sv[:4]) / (np.sum(sv) + 1e-8),
                            float(np.sum(sv > 1e-3)),
                        ]
                    )
                except:
                    feats.extend([0.0] * 5)
            else:
                feats.extend([0.0] * 5)
        else:
            feats.extend([0.0] * 5)
    return np.array(feats, dtype=np.float32)


def train_model_with_checkpoints(loader, model, checkpoint_epochs, total_epochs=6):
    """Train model and extract features at specified checkpoint epochs."""
    model = model.to(device)
    opt = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    sched = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=total_epochs)
    checkpoint_feats = {}
    model.train()
    for ep in range(1, total_epochs + 1):
        for xb, yb in loader:
            xb, yb = xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
            opt.zero_grad()
            loss = criterion(model(xb), yb)
            loss.backward()
            opt.step()
        sched.step()
        if ep in checkpoint_epochs:
            checkpoint_feats[ep] = extract_rich_features(model)
    return model, checkpoint_feats


def build_meta_model(feat_dim, hidden=256):
    return nn.Sequential(
        nn.Linear(feat_dim, hidden),
        nn.LayerNorm(hidden),
        nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Dropout(0.4),
        nn.Linear(hidden, 128),
        nn.LayerNorm(128),
        nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Dropout(0.3),
        nn.Linear(128, 64),
        nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Dropout(0.2),
        nn.Linear(64, 1),
    ).to(device)


def train_meta_kfold(X_norm, y, feat_dim, meta_epochs=150, n_folds=5, seed=42):
    y_min, y_max = y.min(), y.max()
    y_scaled = (y - y_min) / (y_max - y_min + 1e-8)
    kf = KFold(n_splits=n_folds, shuffle=True, random_state=seed)
    fold_test_r2s = []
    all_train_r2 = []
    all_val_r2 = []
    best_fold_data = None

    for fold, (train_idx, test_idx) in enumerate(kf.split(X_norm)):
        X_tr, X_te = X_norm[train_idx], X_norm[test_idx]
        y_tr, y_te = y_scaled[train_idx], y_scaled[test_idx]
        X_tr_t = torch.tensor(X_tr, dtype=torch.float32).to(device)
        y_tr_t = torch.tensor(y_tr, dtype=torch.float32).unsqueeze(1).to(device)
        X_te_t = torch.tensor(X_te, dtype=torch.float32).to(device)
        dl = DataLoader(TensorDataset(X_tr_t, y_tr_t), batch_size=32, shuffle=True)
        meta = build_meta_model(feat_dim)
        opt = optim.Adam(meta.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=5e-4)
        sched = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=meta_epochs)
        crit = nn.MSELoss()
        fold_train_r2s, fold_val_r2s, fold_train_loss, fold_val_loss = [], [], [], []

        for epoch in range(1, meta_epochs + 1):
            meta.train()
            ep_preds, ep_true, ep_loss = [], [], []
            for xb, yb in dl:
                opt.zero_grad()
                pred = meta(xb)
                loss = crit(pred, yb)
                loss.backward()
                opt.step()
                ep_loss.append(loss.item())
                ep_preds.append(pred.detach().cpu().numpy())
                ep_true.append(yb.cpu().numpy())
            sched.step()
            tr2 = r2_score(
                np.concatenate(ep_true).flatten(), np.concatenate(ep_preds).flatten()
            )
            tl = np.mean(ep_loss)
            meta.eval()
            with torch.no_grad():
                vp = meta(X_te_t).cpu().numpy().flatten()
            vl = np.mean((vp - y_te) ** 2)
            vr2 = r2_score(y_te, vp)
            fold_train_r2s.append(tr2)
            fold_val_r2s.append(vr2)
            fold_train_loss.append(tl)
            fold_val_loss.append(vl)

        all_train_r2.append(fold_train_r2s)
        all_val_r2.append(fold_val_r2s)
        meta.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            tp = meta(X_te_t).cpu().numpy().flatten()
        test_r2 = r2_score(y_te, tp)
        fold_test_r2s.append(test_r2)
        test_pred_orig = tp * (y_max - y_min + 1e-8) + y_min
        test_true_orig = y_te * (y_max - y_min + 1e-8) + y_min
        if best_fold_data is None or test_r2 > best_fold_data.get("test_r2", -1):
            best_fold_data = {
                "losses": {"train": fold_train_loss, "val": fold_val_loss},
                "r2_scores": {"train": fold_train_r2s, "val": fold_val_r2s},
                "metrics": {"train": fold_train_r2s, "val": fold_val_r2s},
                "test_r2": test_r2,
                "predictions": test_pred_orig.tolist(),
                "ground_truth": test_true_orig.tolist(),
            }
        print(f"  Fold {fold+1}/{n_folds}: test_r2={test_r2:.4f}")

    mean_r2 = np.mean(fold_test_r2s)
    std_r2 = np.std(fold_test_r2s)
    print(f"  => K-Fold R²: {mean_r2:.4f} ± {std_r2:.4f}")
    best_fold_data["kfold_mean_r2"] = mean_r2
    best_fold_data["kfold_std_r2"] = std_r2
    best_fold_data["all_fold_r2s"] = fold_test_r2s
    best_fold_data["mean_train_r2_by_epoch"] = np.mean(all_train_r2, axis=0).tolist()
    best_fold_data["mean_val_r2_by_epoch"] = np.mean(all_val_r2, axis=0).tolist()
    return best_fold_data

transform_cifar = transforms.Compose(
    [
        transforms.ToTensor(),
        transforms.Normalize((0.4914, 0.4822, 0.4465), (0.2023, 0.1994, 0.2010)),
    ]
)
cifar_train = torchvision.datasets.CIFAR10(
    root="./working/data", train=True, download=True, transform=transform_cifar
)
cifar_labels = np.array(cifar_train.targets)
cifar_class_idx = {c: np.where(cifar_labels == c)[0] for c in range(10)}


def make_cifar_loader(ratio, noise_rate, seed=0):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    minority = list(range(5, 10))
    majority = list(range(0, 5))
    n_maj = 400
    n_min = max(1, int(n_maj / ratio))
    selected, selected_lbl = [], []
    for c in majority:
        idx = cifar_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_maj]
        selected.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(cifar_labels[chosen].tolist())
    for c in minority:
        idx = cifar_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_min]
        selected.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(cifar_labels[chosen].tolist())
    lbls = np.array(selected_lbl)
    n_noisy = int(noise_rate * len(lbls))
    if n_noisy > 0:
        noise_pos = np.random.choice(len(lbls), n_noisy, replace=False)
        lbls[noise_pos] = np.random.randint(0, 10, n_noisy)
    imgs_t = torch.stack([cifar_train[i][0] for i in selected])
    lbls_t = torch.tensor(lbls, dtype=torch.long)
    return DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(imgs_t, lbls_t), batch_size=128, shuffle=True, num_workers=0
    )

NUM_C100 = 100
c100_mean = np.array([0.5071, 0.4867, 0.4408])
c100_std = np.array([0.2675, 0.2565, 0.2761])


def make_c100_loader(noise_rate, ratio, seed=0, n_per_class=35):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    selected_idx, selected_lbl = [], []
    minority_classes = list(range(50, 100))
    majority_classes = list(range(0, 50))
    n_min = max(1, int(n_per_class / ratio))
    for c in majority_classes:
        idx = np.where(c100_lbls == c)[0]
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_per_class]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(c100_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    for c in minority_classes:
        idx = np.where(c100_lbls == c)[0]
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_min]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(c100_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    lbls = np.array(selected_lbl)
    n_noisy = int(noise_rate * len(lbls))
    if n_noisy > 0:
        noise_pos = np.random.choice(len(lbls), n_noisy, replace=False)
        lbls[noise_pos] = np.random.randint(0, NUM_C100, n_noisy)
    imgs_sel = c100_imgs[selected_idx]
    X_t = torch.tensor(imgs_sel, dtype=torch.float32).permute(0, 3, 1, 2) / 255.0
    for c in range(3):
        X_t[:, c] = (X_t[:, c] - c100_mean[c]) / c100_std[c]
    y_t = torch.tensor(lbls, dtype=torch.long)
    return DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(X_t, y_t), batch_size=256, shuffle=True, num_workers=0
    )

def make_fmnist_loader(ratio, noise_rate, seed=0):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    minority = list(range(5, 10))
    majority = list(range(0, 5))
    n_maj = 500
    n_min = max(1, int(n_maj / ratio))
    selected_idx, selected_lbl = [], []
    for c in majority:
        idx = fmnist_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_maj]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(fmnist_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    for c in minority:
        idx = fmnist_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_min]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(fmnist_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    lbls = np.array(selected_lbl)
    n_noisy = int(noise_rate * len(lbls))
    if n_noisy > 0:
        noise_pos = np.random.choice(len(lbls), n_noisy, replace=False)
        lbls[noise_pos] = np.random.randint(0, 10, n_noisy)
    imgs_sel = fmnist_imgs_raw[selected_idx]
    if imgs_sel.ndim == 3:
        imgs_sel = imgs_sel[:, :, :, np.newaxis]
    X_t = torch.tensor(imgs_sel, dtype=torch.float32).permute(0, 3, 1, 2) / 255.0
    X_t = (X_t - fmnist_mean) / fmnist_std
    y_t = torch.tensor(lbls, dtype=torch.long)
    return DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(X_t, y_t), batch_size=128, shuffle=True, num_workers=0
    )

    n_min = max(1, int(n_maj / ratio))
    selected_idx, selected_lbl = [], []
    for c in majority:
        idx = svhn_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_maj]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(svhn_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    for c in minority:
        idx = svhn_class_idx[c].copy()
        np.random.shuffle(idx)
        chosen = idx[:n_min]
        selected_idx.extend(chosen.tolist())
        selected_lbl.extend(svhn_lbls[chosen].tolist())
    lbls = np.array(selected_lbl)
    n_noisy = int(noise_rate * len(lbls))
    if n_noisy > 0:
        noise_pos = np.random.choice(len(lbls), n_noisy, replace=False)
        lbls[noise_pos] = np.random.randint(0, 10, n_noisy)
    imgs_sel = svhn_imgs[selected_idx]
    X_t = torch.tensor(imgs_sel, dtype=torch.float32).permute(0, 3, 1, 2) / 255.0
    for c in range(3):
        X_t[:, c] = (X_t[:, c] - svhn_mean[c]) / svhn_std[c]
    y_t = torch.tensor(lbls, dtype=torch.long)
    return DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(X_t, y_t), batch_size=128, shuffle=True, num_workers=0
    )


np.random.seed(42)
svhn_ratios = np.random.uniform(1.0, 15.0, N_MODELS)
svhn_noises = np.random.uniform(0.0, 0.35, N_MODELS)
svhn_single_feats, svhn_multi_feats = [], []
svhn_ratio_targets, svhn_noise_targets = [], []

t0 = time.time()
for i, (ratio, noise) in enumerate(zip(svhn_ratios, svhn_noises)):
    torch.manual_seed(i + 400)
    loader = make_svhn_loader(ratio, noise, seed=i + 400)
    model = TinyCNN(10, 3)
    _, ckpt_feats = train_model_with_checkpoints(
        loader, model, CHECKPOINT_EPOCHS, TRAIN_EPOCHS
    )
    svhn_single_feats.append(ckpt_feats[CHECKPOINT_EPOCHS[-1]])
    svhn_multi_feats.append(
        np.concatenate([ckpt_feats[ep] for ep in CHECKPOINT_EPOCHS])
    )
    svhn_ratio_targets.append(ratio)
    svhn_noise_targets.append(noise)
    if (i + 1) % 75 == 0:
        print(f"  SVHN: {i+1}/{N_MODELS} | {time.time()-t0:.0f}s")

X_svhn_single = np.stack(svhn_single_feats)
X_svhn_multi = np.stack(svhn_multi_feats)
y_svhn_ratio = np.array(svhn_ratio_targets, dtype=np.float32)
y_svhn_noise = np.array(svhn_noise_targets, dtype=np.float32)
X_svhn_single_n = (X_svhn_single - X_svhn_single.mean(0)) / (
    X_svhn_single.std(0) + 1e-8
)
X_svhn_multi_n = (X_svhn_multi - X_svhn_multi.mean(0)) / (X_svhn_multi.std(0) + 1e-8)

print("SVHN Imbalance - Single Checkpoint:")
r = train_meta_kfold(
    X_svhn_single_n, y_svhn_ratio, X_svhn_single_n.shape[1], META_EPOCHS, N_FOLDS
)
experiment_data["single_checkpoint"]["svhn_imbalance"] = r

print("SVHN Imbalance - Multi Checkpoint:")
r = train_meta_kfold(
    X_svhn_multi_n, y_svhn_ratio, X_svhn_multi_n.shape[1], META_EPOCHS, N_FOLDS
)
experiment_data["multi_checkpoint"]["svhn_imbalance"] = r

print("SVHN Noise - Single Checkpoint:")
r = train_meta_kfold(
    X_svhn_single_n, y_svhn_noise, X_svhn_single_n.shape[1], META_EPOCHS, N_FOLDS
)
experiment_data["single_checkpoint"]["svhn_noise"] = r

print("SVHN Noise - Multi Checkpoint:")
r = train_meta_kfold(
    X_svhn_multi_n, y_svhn_noise, X_svhn_multi_n.shape[1], META_EPOCHS, N_FOLDS
)
experiment_data["multi_checkpoint"]["svhn_noise"] = r

np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "svhn_single_features.npy"), X_svhn_single)
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "svhn_multi_features.npy"), X_svhn_multi)

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Save experiment data
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

task_keys = [
    ("cifar10_imbalance", "CIFAR-10\nImbalance"),
    ("cifar10_noise", "CIFAR-10\nNoise"),
    ("cifar100_imbalance", "CIFAR-100\nImbalance"),
    ("cifar100_noise", "CIFAR-100\nNoise"),
    ("fashionmnist_imbalance", "FashionMNIST\nImbalance"),
    ("fashionmnist_noise", "FashionMNIST\nNoise"),
    ("svhn_imbalance", "SVHN\nImbalance"),
    ("svhn_noise", "SVHN\nNoise"),
]

single_r2 = [
    experiment_data["single_checkpoint"][k]["kfold_mean_r2"] for k, _ in task_keys
]
single_std = [
    experiment_data["single_checkpoint"][k]["kfold_std_r2"] for k, _ in task_keys
]
multi_r2 = [
    experiment_data["multi_checkpoint"][k]["kfold_mean_r2"] for k, _ in task_keys
]
multi_std = [
    experiment_data["multi_checkpoint"][k]["kfold_std_r2"] for k, _ in task_keys
]

print("=== K-Fold Mean R² Results ===")
for (k, label), s, m in zip(task_keys, single_r2, multi_r2):
    print(
        f"{label.replace(chr(10),' ')}: Single={s:.4f}, Multi={m:.4f}, Gain={m-s:+.4f}"
    )
print(f"\nAvg Single R²: {np.mean(single_r2):.4f}")
print(f"Avg Multi R²:  {np.mean(multi_r2):.4f}")
print(f"Avg Gain:      {np.mean([m-s for m,s in zip(multi_r2,single_r2)]):+.4f}")

# Plot 1: Bar chart comparing single vs multi R²
try:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 7))

except Exception as e:

# Plot 2: R² gain (multi - single)
try:

# Plot 3: Learning curves for all 8 tasks (val R²)
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(20, 10))
    for ax, (key, label) in zip(axes.flatten(), task_keys):

# Plot 4: Scatter pred vs true for all 8 tasks (best checkpoint mode per task)
try:

        ax.scatter(gt, pr, alpha=0.5, color=color, s=25)
        mn, mx = min(gt.min(), pr.min()), max(gt.max(), pr.max())
        ax.plot([mn, mx], [mn, mx], "r--", lw=1.5)
        ax.set_title(
            f'{label.replace(chr(10), " ")}\n{mode_label} | R²={data["kfold_mean_r2"]:.3f}',
            fontsize=8,
        )
        ax.set_xlabel("True", fontsize=8)
        ax.set_ylabel("Predicted", fontsize=8)
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.4)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Predicted vs True Values (Best Checkpoint Mode per Task)\nLeft: Ground Truth axis, Bottom: Predicted axis",
        fontsize=12,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "all_datasets_pred_vs_true_best_mode.png"),
        dpi=100,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: all_datasets_pred_vs_true_best_mode.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot 4: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 5: Box plot of per-fold R² across tasks
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(18, 7))
    for ax, mode, color, title in zip(
        axes,
        ["single_checkpoint", "multi_checkpoint"],
        ["steelblue", "darkorange"],
        ["Single Checkpoint (ep6)", "Multi Checkpoint (ep1+3+6)"],
    ):
        all_fold_r2s = [experiment_data[mode][k]["all_fold_r2s"] for k, _ in task_keys]
        bp = ax.boxplot(
            all_fold_r2s,
            patch_artist=True,
            notch=False,
            boxprops=dict(facecolor=color, alpha=0.6),
            medianprops=dict(color="black", lw=2),
        )
        ax.set_xticks(range(1, len(task_keys) + 1))
        ax.set_xticklabels([label for _, label in task_keys], fontsize=9)
        ax.set_ylabel("Test R² per Fold", fontsize=11)
        ax.set_title(f"{title}\nPer-Fold R² Distribution Across Tasks", fontsize=10)
        ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.4)
        ax.set_ylim(-0.3, 1.1)
        means = [np.mean(r) for r in all_fold_r2s]
        ax.plot(
            range(1, len(task_keys) + 1),
            means,
            "r^",
            markersize=7,
            label="Mean R²",
            zorder=5,
        )
        ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    plt.suptitle(
        "K-Fold R² Score Distributions: Single vs Multi-Checkpoint Features\nAll Datasets and Tasks",
        fontsize=12,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "all_datasets_kfold_r2_boxplot.png"),
        dpi=100,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: all_datasets_kfold_r2_boxplot.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot 5: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 6: Summary heatmap of R² values
try:
    datasets = ["CIFAR-10", "CIFAR-100", "FashionMNIST", "SVHN"]
    tasks_short = ["Imbalance", "Noise"]
    data_matrix_single = np.array(
        [[single_r2[i * 2], single_r2[i * 2 + 1]] for i in range(4)]
    )
    data_matrix_multi = np.array(
        [[multi_r2[i * 2], multi_r2[i * 2 + 1]] for i in range(4)]
    )
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))
    for ax, matrix, title in zip(
        axes,
        [data_matrix_single, data_matrix_multi],
        ["Single Checkpoint (ep6)", "Multi Checkpoint (ep1+3+6)"],
    ):
        im = ax.imshow(matrix, cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=0, vmax=1, aspect="auto")
        ax.set_xticks([0, 1])
        ax.set_xticklabels(tasks_short, fontsize=11)
        ax.set_yticks(range(4))
        ax.set_yticklabels(datasets, fontsize=11)
        ax.set_title(title, fontsize=11)
        plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="R²")
        for i in range(4):
            for j in range(2):
                ax.text(
                    j,
                    i,
                    f"{matrix[i,j]:.3f}",
                    ha="center",
                    va="center",
                    fontsize=12,
                    fontweight="bold",
                    color="black" if 0.3 < matrix[i, j] < 0.8 else "white",
                )
    plt.suptitle(
        "R² Score Heatmap: Single vs Multi-Checkpoint Features\nLeft: Single Checkpoint, Right: Multi Checkpoint",
        fontsize=12,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "all_datasets_r2_heatmap.png"),
        dpi=100,
